methods.description: The process of determining the abundance of pollen produced by flowers using the ether method, modified by Szklanowska.

steps.description: Flowers for pollen samples were collected just before corolla opening, 3 times during the blooming period. Each time 4 samples were gathered. A single sample contained 50 mature anthers.
steps.searchable_summary: collect flower samples for pollen analysis

steps.description: The abundance of pollen produced by flowers was determined by the ether method, modified by Szklanowska.
steps.searchable_summary: determine pollen abundance using modified ether method

methods.description: Checking pollen viability and measuring the length of the equatorial and polar axis of pollen grains.

steps.description: Pollen viability was checked in standard semi-permanent slides stained with acetocarmine.
steps.searchable_summary: check pollen viability using acetocarmine staining

steps.description: Measurements of the length of the equatorial and polar axis were taken in equatorial view of pollen grains, mounted in glycerin-jelly slides.
steps.searchable_summary: measure equatorial and polar axis of pollen grains

steps.description: Pollen grains were classified according to Erdtman (1956) based on shape and size.
steps.searchable_summary: classify pollen grains by shape and size

methods.description: Evaluation of the abundance of flowering by counting freshly opened flowers on randomly chosen branches.

steps.description: Each year freshly opened flowers were marked and counted on a randomly chosen branch of eight shrubs of the investigated cultivars. The counting was performed every 2-3 days during the blooming period.
steps.searchable_summary: count freshly opened flowers on selected branches

steps.description: The number of branches per each shrub was determined and the flower number per plant was calculated.

methods.description: Statistical analysis of the collected data using two-way ANOVA followed by Duncan test.

steps.description: The data were analyzed by two-way ANOVA followed by Duncan test at α= 0.05.
steps.searchable_summary: perform two-way ANOVA and Duncan test on data
